rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRIAN EUGENE CAMPBELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order. The Court waives notice of the erroneously captioned "supplement amendment" filed on 12/7/23. (A supplement provides additional information not disclosed in a verified pleading. An amendment modifies a pleading or requests relief that differs from what was requested in the modified pleading. [CRC 7.3.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 2-1/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. The ONLY proper form of notice in probate proceedings is one of the mandatory Judicial Council forms, depending on the relief sought. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to the proof of service with the instructions for how to appear remotely.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PAUL SAM TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 5, 2019, AS RESTATED on March 18, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing And Demurrer To Amended Objection Of Jennifer Sam To The Petition For Order Determining Invalidity Of Purported Trust And Cross-Petition Of Jennifer Sam To Determine Validity Of Trust Sections 3.2 And 5.3(B) Of The Paul Sam Trust Dated November 5, 2019
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Petitioner Diana Sam's Demurrer to Amended Objection of Jennifer Sam to the Petition for Order Determining Invalidity of Purported Trust and Cross-Petition of Jennifer Sam to Determine Validity of Trust Sections 3.2 and 5.3(b) of the Paul Sam Trust dated November 5, 2019. Respondent's Request for Judicial Notice of the Amended Objection of Jennifer Sam to the Petition for Order Determining Invalidity of Purported Trust and Cross-Petition of Jennifer Sam to Determine Validity of Trust Sections 3.2 and 5.3(b) of the Paul Sam Trust dated November 5, 2019 is GRANTED. Per the Notice of Hearing, the Demurrer is brought on the grounds that the Cross-Petition fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for Undue Influence, Elder Abuse, Breach of Fiduciary Duty, and Constructive Fraud, respectively. There are four causes of action pleaded in the Cross-Petition: 1) "Invalidity of Appointment as Trustee in Section 1.1 and Invalidity Sections 3.2 and 5.3(B) of The 2019 Instrument for Reason of Undue Influence;" 2) "Elder Abuse;" 3) "Breach of Fiduciary Duty;" and 4) "Constructive Fraud." The Cross-Petition portion of the Amended Objection of Jennifer Sam to the Petition for Order Determining Invalidity of Purported Trust and Cross-Petition of Jennifer Sam to Determine Validity of Trust Sections 3.2 and 5.3(b) of the Paul Sam Trust dated November 5, 2019 begins at 14:13 of the pleading. At paragraph 79, "Objector hereby incorporates the precedent paragraphs as though set forth fully herein." The "precedent paragraphs" constitute the Amended Objection of Jennifer Sam to the Petition for Order Determining Invalidity of Purported Trust. The pleading also references declarations, some of which are not filed as part of or in support of the pleading, but were filed in support of other pleadings in this matter. When considering a demurrer, the trial court must accept as true all material facts pleaded in the complaint and those arising by reasonable implication. (Moore v. Conliffe (1994) 7 Cal. 4th 634, 638; Montclair Parkowners Ass'n v. City of Montclair (1999) 76 Cal. App. 4th 784.) This does not apply to allegations expressing mere conclusions of law, or allegations contradicted by the exhibits to the complaint or by matters of which judicial notice may be taken. (Vance v. Villa Park Mobilehome Estates (1995) 36 Cal. App. 4th 698, 709.) A demurrer is a pleading used to challenge the legal sufficiency of an opponent's pleading based on defects that appear either on the face of the pleading under attack or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable. (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal. 3d 311, 318; County of Fresno v. Shelton (1998) 66 Cal. App. 4th 996, 1008, 1009.) A demurrer must demonstrate no right to any relief under the cause of action. (See, Augustine v. Trucco (1954) 124 Cal.App.2d 229, 236.) However, Petitioner's Demurrer to the First Cause Of Action: Invalidity of Appointment as Trustee in Section 1.1 and Invalidity Sections 3.2 and 5.3(B) of The 2019 Instrument for Reason of Undue Influence on the grounds of failure to allege facts showing necessary elements of "influence" exerted by Petitioner over Paul Sam in obtaining the disputed portions of the 2019 Trust, and Petitioner's "participation" in development of the 2019 Trust is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Petitioner's demurrer to the Second Cause of Action: Elder Abuse on the grounds of failure to allege facts showing Petitioner "took" or "obtained" any "real or personal property" from Paul Sam or that Petitioner otherwise "deprived" Paul Sam of any property rights is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PAUL SAM TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 5, 2019, AS RESTATED on March 18, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Demurrer To Amended Objection Of Jennifer Sam To The Petition For Order Determining Invalidity Of Purported Trust And Cross-Petition Of Jennifer Sam To Determine Validity Of Trust Sections 3.2 And 5.3(B) Of The Paul Sam Trust Dated November 5, 2019 (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] Petitioner's demurrer to the Third Cause of Action: Breach of Fiduciary Duty on the grounds of failure to allege facts showing Petitioner had a fiduciary duty to Paul Sam at the relevant time or that Petitioner breached any alleged fiduciary duty at the relevant time is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Petitioner's demurrer to the Fourth Cause of Action: Constructive Fraud on the grounds of failure to allege facts showing Petitioner had a fiduciary duty to Paul Sam at the relevant time or that Petitioner breached any alleged fiduciary duty at the relevant time is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. The factual allegations presented in the Opposition to Demurrer are either not presented in the Cross-Petition or are presented more clearly in the Opposition as factual allegations rather than conclusions of law as in the Cross-Petition. Since there is a reasonable possibility that the Cross-Petition could be amended to cure the defects now subject to demurrer, leave to amend must be granted (see, Galbiso v. Orosi Pub. Util. Dist. (2010) 182 Cal. App. 4th 652, 674). Petitioner Jennifer Sam is directed to file the amended petition as a separate, stand-alone pleading, within 20 days of Notice of Entry of this Order. Respondent Diana Sam shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]
